title: Similar to an Apple Watch, with major trade offs
text: Watch isn’t bad at all. I bought this as a gift for my partner and it seems to work for him. Interface is just like an Apple Watch would look. Seems to do an okay job tracking stuff. It also holds a charge seemingly well. It’s hard to attach the strap to the watch though and pops out a lot. My biggest complaint is that if you leave anything but a 4 or 5 star review, the company will contact you relentlessly about offering you money if you change your review. I’ve continued receiving these emails every week. Feels pretty dishonest to me as a consumer that most reviews are bought.

title: Great Price!
text: Got a great price during Cyber Monday on this watch and I'm very glad I did! It looks great, has a bunch of good features and fits my small wrists well at the second to smallest setting. I'm very excited to give it a try after my $300 MK watch crapped out super fast.

title: Won’t track anything
text: Received this watch and it connected to my phone via Bluetooth immediately. Unfortunately, it won’t track most things it’s supposed to! My steps, BP, temperature won’t track at all. If I put the watch skin tight it will read my heart rate and oxygen, but  if I put the band on comfortably, it won’t read them. The screen only lights up for 4 seconds, it’s not enough time to look at what I’m trying to do. There’s no way to adjust how long it lights up for, so I have to keep tapping the screen to keep it lit up while I try to see if it’s measuring anything. The instructions say it’s supposed to light up automatically if you move your arm up, but it will not. You have to press the side button every time to have the screen light up. The temperature is stuck at 3°C, even though it’s currently 40° where I’m at.

title: Excellent battery life, easy to read and lots of features
text: This Smartwatch has excellent battery life and recharges quickly. The battery lasted for an entire week on a single charge without turning it off. It is easy to turn off at night if you don't need sleep tracking. The watch's face is bright but can easily be adjusted for lower power consumption. It is easy to create a watch face with a custom picture (see attached picture) but it has limited customization data display.  There are a number of watch faces that can be displayed but not as many as fancy watches.  The fitness tracking seems adequate (and sometimes impressive) but not always intuitive (which is personal/cultural.) The connection to my Pixel watch was solid but I had to read and follow the instructions carefully to get it to work. I liked this watch so much that bought one for my wife and my son, too.

title: Pretty good watch
text: This is my first "smart watch" so I'm still getting used to it.  The biggest user issue I have is that it isn't simply ON when I look at it.  It usually turns on if I move my arm properly, and I can switch it ON using my other hand.  But, when in busy or messy tasks, that's not the best thing.  The sleep tracker may or may not be really accurate....the step tracker is running at about 50% of my Fitbit pocket unit.  I'm thinking that the watch only really counts steps if my arm is free and swinging.  So, walking a half mile, pushing a shopping cart sounds as zero steps.  Pulse and blood oxygen seem to be close anyway.  But, the band is comfortable, and doesn't have that slimy silicone feel that some do.  Big numbers on the face and easy to read, and lots of faces available or put your own image up from your phone library.  Overall good value in my humble opinion.  Oh.....battery life is amazing.  Just charged it today because it was convenient.  But, it had been running on last week's c

title: It's Okay (Update - No It’s Not)
text:
[[VIDEOID:5baa7187a34a9409fa19b02d75d65c6f]] Update - Changed from 3 to 1 star. This watch is crap and gets worse by the day. At least now I have video of it being possessed, changing screens on its own, making selections, turning off my alarm, etc. I can’t tell you how many times I’ve taken the stupid thing off in a meeting or while I’m laying still to go to sleep because it continually buzzes at me to change the screen face. I’m super over it and already ordered something new, this watch has beyond pissed me off.

I was looking for a smartwatch replacement when my FitBit started fizzling out. Found this product on sale and it had decent reviews. The watch is okay, it does the bare minimum of what I was looking for (steps, sleep, HR, notifications, etc.) without paying hundreds of dollars. That's all I needed, and the app is fine too.

Now after about 3 months it's starting to act kind of stupid. It's really hard to get the screen to wake when you lift your arm up.

title: Updated review stopped connecting to my iPhone after a week
text:
I was originally excited about this smart watch due to the size of the face. I have a Fitbit and wanted a bigger looking face. Although I love the options for the changing of the face as you can see in the photo it's very inaccurate when reading daily steps. I tested it my second day and wore my Fitbit and then this watch all day long. This smart watch over estimated my steps by almost 1500 steps which is quite a lot. If you don't mind deducting those steps everyday then you will be fine. I do like the fact it connected to my iPhone quickly and when notifications are turned on I can see emails and text message so that is a nice feature. I use my Fitbit to sleep with and track my sleep as this watch feels to big to wear to bed all night. Overall I will keep the watch with it's flaws as it has other good features but if you are specifically looking for accurate account of daily steps maybe get a Fitbit

Updated this is a piece of crap returning it!

title: Nope
text:
Edited after using it for workouts: I do not have a heart condition yet while on the elliptical my heart rate went up and down between 65-125. Usually when I am sitting down this watch says 65-110. I ended up checking my own heart rate manually and this watch is so off. What's the point of wearing it if it is innaccurate for so many things? I do not recommend.

Original 3 star review: This is my first fitness watch ever. I didn't want to spend too much because I didn't know if I would even like having one at all. I guess it does what it is supposed to for the most part. I don't use it for calls or texts. So I can't comment on those functions. No desire to be Dick Tracy. The sleep tracking is inaccurate. I can be up and moving and it will still act like I am asleep. The only way I can seem to stop it is to continue walking around or marching in place. Which is stupid. Everything else seems to work fine. But I'm also not a very active person yet. I don't have a good gym routin

title: Don’t trust for your health
text: Used while on the treadmill after installing app and connecting to phone. Took 10 minutes before a heart rate would show up. Then it would keep the same reading even when I went from a 3mph walking pace to 7mph jogging speed. Then eventually it would show a higher heart rate . If you want this to ensure you don’t over exert yourself then do not buy it. You could have a heart attack and it wouldn’t tell you til it’s too late. I knew I shouldn’t have tried a cheap model

title: Poor Product
text: Product is worthless.  I haven’t resolved is the text message alerts, otherwise watch is a worthless weight. Not precise with stepping counts etc Overall, quite unsatisfied with features of this Chinese made scam

title: Broke within a week
text: My sons watch broke with in a week I reached out to the company and they have a spamish looking email did everything they requested and have yet to receive a replacement.

title: Works as described
text: Watch works as described.  Battery lasts a week or so and charges really quickly.  I don't keep my bluetooth on, I just turn it on periodically to refresh the app with new data.

title: Works for the price
text:
I’ll start with the one good thing which was that the app seemed to work fine with my iPhone

Aside from that, the low price point is the only reason I don’t give it 1 star. The watch doesn’t monitor heart rate unless you’re actually going to the heart rate menu and having it measure it. The only exception was when I was running—however it’s here where the watch was even less reliable. It supposedly tracked my heart rate but gave a maximum of 72. I’m not a fast runner or an elite athlete. The heart rate was way off. Every other watch always gets into the 100-140bpm range.
I didn’t feel the sleep monitor was highly accurate but I couldn’t say for sure.
The touch response was not all that great—for instance I had trouble scrolling through fitness activities

For the price, if you want a simple step counter with the time then it’s good. Otherwise I felt it was not accurate and essentially a waste whether you’re just starting a fitness regimen or are al

title: Inaccurate and misleading
text: I received this watch yesterday. I slept in it last night to try out the sleep tracking. This morning, I got up, made coffee, sat down to drink it and looked at the watch and the app. According to that, I was still sleeping! Clearly, I wasn’t. So today I went for a short run. I decided to wear my Garmin watch, since I use it connected to the Strava app. I left this watch at home on my bathroom counter. When I came home, I put it back on. Then I opened the app to look at it and I was very surprised and confused to see that the watch had continued to record heart rates and blood pressure during the time I had left it at home and nobody was wearing it!!! I also own a blood pressure cuff… so I compared that measurement to the watch and they don’t match. Neither does the heart rate. I feel like the readings are completely bogus!!! I’m disappointed! Additionally, I put my cat’s photo on the screen to personalize it, and it was on there all day yesterday but it mysteriously disappeared and no

title: DON'T BUY!
text: This just shows random numbers.  There's no way to set the time and date, the blood pressure, heart rate, blood oxygen, etc. are just random, they go up and down but I checked against a real blood pressure cuff and it was off by 20, the heart rate was about 15 above and then 15 below my actual pulse, too.  The number of steps is random too - one minute it's over 900 the next it's 68.  It's a piece of garbage, and when I tried to return it for being defective, Amazon said it is not eligible for returns!  So I say again, DO NOT BUY THIS!!!!

title: Replacing the wife's Fitbit that won't keep accurate time-  great product
text: great pricing on reliable and quality products.  I have several of their EarPods as well.  Continues to impress me with features, quality and customer service on all their products I have tried.  Can't go wrong with this one.

title: Terrible
text: Have been using for a few weeks. The sensors aren't accurate...it doesn't reset when you want unless you delete all settings, so I cannot properly monitor my workout. When driving it is constantly lighting up due to the motion sensor and counts steps when driving. Very limited info coming from this. pretty much the only thing it is good for is to tell time. Worthless Garbage

title: Junk
text: Could not pair device. Got no where with customer service,could find tech support. Total waste of time and money. Kept saying need app to pair this up. Had the app,still nothing. Junk junk junk.

title: Great Affordable Smart Watch Features
text:
This is an affordable smart watch packed with features. It charges easily with the included charger and the battery lasted a few days. It does take a bit of playing around with to figure out the settings but once you do, you won’t be disappointed. The app you access by scanning the code with your phone.
The screen is large and easy to read. The band is comfortable and easy to clean if you are wearing it for sports activities. The music and health features alone make it a great gift for teens.

title: App muy mala
text: El reloj funciona muy bien, no tengo quejas de el, pero la aplicación es un verdedero desastre, porque constantemente se desconecta, no tiene sentido que esté vinculado al teléfono por el Bluetooth para recibir llamadas y notificaciones, y constantemente se desconecta, y después es un problema para volverlo a vincular, el reloj es bueno para el precio, pero la aplicación le resta todo eso valor, es una pena.

title: Meets expectations.
text: Easy to set up and use. Does all I needed it to do.

title: Great Buy
text: Easy to use and accurate. Specially happy with the battery life ! Did not expected so many features at just $47. Very happy with the purchase

title: Easy to read
text: I've had a tracker before and this one offers so many other options. It has a good battery life. Easy to read, although has a small learning curve for setup. Great value

title: Great Features
text: Can't believe the features in this watch. Excellent value. Comfortable band.
